context: You are generating or enriching entries for the Japanese words collection.
Work only on the requested workflow step.
outputRequirements: Follow the active step's field rules exactly.
rules: Match the concise dictionary style of the collection.
Prefer common useful vocabulary, but keep specialized item types consistent with the collection when requested.
Keep English meanings short and learner-friendly.
Use `semanticClass` and `tags` only when they add clear value.

steps.title: Create Word Entries
steps.goal: Create concise dictionary-style Japanese word entries from the provided input.
inputContract: The request may provide Japanese words, English meanings, categories, item types, or a scenario.

fieldRules.description: Canonical dictionary/headword kanji form if one exists. If no established kanji exists, use the standard kana or katakana headword.
Pronunciation written in kana. Usually hiragana, but katakana is allowed for katakana words.
noun, proper-noun, morpheme, ichidan-verb, godan-verb, irregular-verb, i-adjective, na-adjective, particle, adverb, counter, expression, numeral, descriptive-phrase, phrase-sentence, conjunction
Optional semantic grouping such as onomatopoeia, greeting, food, city, or sfx.
Optional machine-friendly tags. Use short snake_case or existing collection-style tags only when useful.

instructions: Keep `kanji` as the canonical headword field even when the most common display form is kana or katakana.
Use `morpheme` only for bound elements mainly used inside compounds rather than normal standalone words.
Use `expression`, `descriptive-phrase`, or `phrase-sentence` for fixed multi-word units when a single-word type would be misleading.
For verb entries, use dictionary form.
For adjectives, distinguish carefully between `i-adjective` and `na-adjective`.
For onomatopoeia and sound-symbolic words, usually use `semanticClass` like `onomatopoeia` or `sfx` when appropriate.

steps.title: Extract Headwords
steps.goal: Extract useful learnable Japanese headwords from the provided text before entry creation.
inputContract: The request provides Japanese text such as a sentence, passage, or short dialogue.
Return only a JSON array of canonical Japanese headword strings.

fieldRules.description: A useful canonical Japanese headword suitable for later dictionary entry creation.

instructions: Prefer useful headwords over exhaustive extraction.
Skip particles and trivial function-word-only extraction unless they are intentionally being studied.
Prefer canonical dictionary forms over inflected surface forms.

expectedState: The result is a clean list of learnable headwords ready for entry creation.

prompts.details: Create entries for the provided Japanese words.
Create Japanese entries from the provided English meanings.
Create Japanese entries in the provided categories.
Create Japanese entries for the provided item types.
Create Japanese entries needed for the provided scenario.
prompts.example: "shopping at a convenience store in Japan"

prompts.details: Extract useful headwords from Japanese text before later entry creation.
